{"key_event":"Popular Front mobilization and Depression-era executive decree-laws","reasoning":"Civil society remained very strong and became more intensely organized through unions, parties, leagues, strikes, and anti-fascist mobilization, though polarization and the 1938 strike repression limited the net gain. The state retained a strong administrative and coercive apparatus and expanded regulation, finance, and defense controls, but Depression-era fiscal weakness, cabinet instability, and legitimacy crises pulled it below the late-1920s level.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":8.5,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":8.6}
